title: leucas glabrata var. chiatelliniana
intro: Leucas glabrata var. chiatelliniana, also known as 'Nagakesar' or 'Nagchampa', is a plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae and is native to tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its various health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in essential oil form, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits. Additionally, Leucas glabrata var. chiatelliniana is also valued for its fragrance and is used in perfumery and aromatherapy applications.
